mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway that excels in performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, providing advanced features like load balancing and authentication. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential complexity in managing plugins.

steps.title: Install the Kong CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kong command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing Kong Gateway.

```bash

curl -L https://bit.ly/kong-gateway-cli -o kong-cli.tar.gz

tar -xzf kong-cli.tar.gz && sudo mv kong /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kong CLI tarball.
> Extract and move the CLI to your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster where Kong Gateway will be deployed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30.0

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
This step installs Kong Gateway using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Update your Helm repositories.
> Create a namespace for Kong.
> Install Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures essential features like autoscaling and monitoring for Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kubernetes/kong-ingress.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kubernetes/kong-autoscale.yaml

```

> Apply the ingress configuration.
> Set up autoscaling for Kong.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kong Gateway from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstall Kong from the 'kong' namespace.
> Delete the Kong namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Drain the Kong pods.
> Upgrade Kong to the specified version.
> Verify that the Kong pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress controller not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress controller may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kong' to check the status of the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The database connection settings may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify your database configuration in the Kong values file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway may not be properly deployed or the service is down.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kong pods with 'kubectl get pods -n kong'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Ingress not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ingress resource may not be created or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the ingress resource is defined correctly and applied.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-LLM traffic management, enhanced plugin architecture.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.8, review the new plugin configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all Kong pods in 'Running' status when everything is working.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
